skill.name: plannotator
skill.description: Interactive plan and diff review for AI coding agents with visual browser UI for annotating plans, approving or requesting changes with structured feedback, and auto-save to Obsidian or Bear Notes.
skill.summary: Visual plan review and code annotation tool for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode with structured feedback loops.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Legitimate plan review tool for AI coding agents. Static analysis flagged 380 patterns across 10 files, but most are false positives for standard installer/hook functionality. The curl | bash installation pattern presents supply chain risk but is common practice. All filesystem access targets standard AI tool config locations. No credential exfiltration or command injection vectors detected.

medium_findings.title: Pipe to Shell Installation Pattern
medium_findings.description: The install.sh script uses curl | bash pattern to download and execute remote scripts. This presents supply chain risk if the remote server is compromised. Commands are executed from plannotator.ai/install.sh without integrity verification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of curl piped to bash without hash verification - classic supply chain attack vector, though commonly used for convenience.

low_findings.title: AI Tool Configuration File Modification
low_findings.description: Scripts modify configuration files for Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex CLI. This is intended functionality but grants persistent access to AI tool behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Files explicitly target AI tool config locations with hardcoded JSON paths - legitimate hook setup but persistent modification of AI behavior.

low_findings.title: Remote Shell Configuration
low_findings.description: configure-remote.sh modifies shell profile files (.zshrc, .bashrc) and SSH configuration for remote mode. This is intended for devcontainer/WSL support but modifies persistent shell environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Script modifies shell profiles for remote development support - legitimate use case but persistent environment modification warrants user awareness.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Installation downloads and executes scripts from remote server without integrity checks. Users should verify the source before running.

content.user_title: Review AI Agent Plans Visually Before Coding
content.value_statement: Catch implementation mistakes before code is written by reviewing AI agent plans in a visual browser interface. Annotate plans with structured feedback, approve or request changes, and auto-save to Obsidian or Bear Notes.
actual_capabilities: Opens visual browser UI when AI agents exit plan mode for interactive plan review
Annotates plans with delete, insert, replace, and comment markers for structured feedback
Reviews git diffs after implementation with unified or split view comparison
Auto-saves approved plans to Obsidian vaults with YAML frontmatter and tags
Integrates with Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and OpenCode via hooks or plugins
Supports remote development via SSH port forwarding and devcontainer mode
limitations: Requires browser access for visual UI - headless or automated browser sessions cannot configure settings
Export and Notes save features only work in hook mode, not in standalone CLI review mode
Bear Notes integration requires macOS or iOS - not available on Linux or Windows
Settings persist in browser cookies, not localStorage - different browser profiles do not share configuration

use_cases.title: Pre-Implementation Plan Review
use_cases.description: Review AI agent implementation plans before any code is written. Annotate risky steps, add missing requirements, and approve only when the approach aligns with project goals.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering leads managing AI-assisted development teams

use_cases.title: Post-Implementation Code Review
use_cases.description: Review git diffs after AI agents complete coding. Annotate specific line ranges with expected behavior changes and attach screenshots for UI feedback.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Claude Code or Codex for rapid prototyping

use_cases.title: Team Knowledge Sharing
use_cases.description: Share annotated plan reviews with teammates via link. Auto-save approved plans to Obsidian for team documentation and future reference.
use_cases.target_user: Technical architects documenting system design decisions

prompt_templates.title: Basic Plan Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this implementation plan and annotate any steps that seem risky or incomplete. Mark steps to delete, insert, or replace with specific feedback.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user reviewing a simple feature plan

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this plan focusing on architectural implications. Check for: database schema changes, API contract modifications, backward compatibility, and migration requirements. Add comments for any missing considerations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Senior developer reviewing a complex refactor plan

prompt_templates.title: Security Review Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this plan for security concerns. Verify: input validation, authentication checks, authorization boundaries, data sanitization, and logging requirements. Request changes for any security gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Team Review Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a plan review session that can be shared with the team. Include: project context, acceptance criteria, performance requirements, and testing strategy. Enable Obsidian auto-save for documentation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Tech lead preparing a plan for team alignment

output_examples.input: AI agent submits a plan to add user authentication with JWT tokens
output: Plan opened in browser UI at localhost:19432
Reviewer annotates step 3: 'Replace - Add rate limiting to login endpoint'
Reviewer adds comment: 'Include password complexity requirements'
Reviewer clicks Approve - agent proceeds with implementation

output_examples.input: Developer runs plannotator review after agent completes coding
output: Git diff loaded with 5 file changes
Line 42-48 annotated: 'This validation should happen before database write'
Image attached showing expected UI layout
Feedback exported to markdown for agent reference

best_practices: Review plans before implementation starts to catch wrong approaches early and save iteration time
Keep each annotation tied to one concrete actionable change for clarity
Include acceptance criteria when requesting changes so the agent knows what success looks like
anti_patterns: Approving plans without reading - defeats the purpose of catching mistakes before coding
Vague annotations like 'fix this' without explaining what change is needed
Requesting major scope changes after approval - create a new plan review instead

faq.question: How do I install plannotator for Claude Code?
faq.answer: Run ./install.sh --with-plugin for CLI and plugin commands, then run /plugin marketplace add backnotprop/plannotator inside Claude Code. Alternatively run ./setup-hook.sh for manual hook configuration. Restart Claude Code after setup.

faq.question: Does plannotator work with Gemini CLI and Codex CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. Run ./setup-gemini-hook.sh for Gemini CLI or ./setup-codex-hook.sh for Codex CLI. You can also run ./install.sh --all to configure all AI tool integrations at once.

faq.question: How does the Obsidian auto-save feature work?
faq.answer: Enable Obsidian integration in Settings gear icon then Saving tab. Select your vault from the dropdown. Approved plans auto-save with YAML frontmatter including tags for project name, title words, and detected languages.

faq.question: Can I use plannotator on a remote server or devcontainer?
faq.answer: Yes. Run ./configure-remote.sh to set up SSH port forwarding. Set PLANNOTATOR_REMOTE=1 to disable auto-browser-open and PLANNOTATOR_PORT to use a fixed port. Forward the port via SSH and access via browser.

faq.question: What happens if I deny a plan?
faq.answer: Your annotations are sent back as structured feedback to the AI agent. The agent can then revise the plan based on your comments and resubmit for another review cycle.

faq.question: Do I need Obsidian to use plannotator?
faq.answer: No. Obsidian integration is optional. You can review and approve plans without it. Auto-save can be enabled later via the Settings UI if you install Obsidian.
